pages.text:
procession for __Palm Sunday__ outside the
headmaster's house. We sang hymns
at a few places + reached the church
which was welcoming. Thomas left a
message on the mobile phone to pick up
Matt Moon + him at 1.45.
    After ~~lunch~~ ~~a~~ church we had coffees
in the van + saw a bit of Oundle.
After lunch at the Coffee Tavern we picked up
Thomas + Matt Moon at the school. We have
set off through rainstorms for home. Matt is
leaving at New St. Sta. for Bristd.
    April 7th.
    Peter, John, Charles, Eve left for
__Beauchamp__ at 8.30. am. John is on a
music course. After dropping John we
went to call on Wendy King. They were house
tidying. The children played with the children in
the garden + walked. Eve had 2 cups of coffee.
Then Wendy led us to Robert Hammond's
house at __Taynton__. Apparently Gus King +

Mark Hammond are sworn rivals on the village cricket teams which share the same field. We had more coffee.

The field is so boggy that we are camping on the carpark. John's course finishes at 4. + then the boys played football + ping pong.

__April 8th__
    Having packed John off to music, Peter + Charles + Eve went to pick up Robert to shop in Gloucester. He found a good carpark for us. Then we went bad for a long, comfortable lunch with Liz + Mark + Victoria + Louisa. Mark's father wins prizes with his wine.
    It started raining as we set off to Beauchamp, + was a wet evening. Simon joined us at 8.15. for a late supper.

__April 9th__
    We went to the Waterways Museum at Gloucester. It is very cold +

captions: Inland Waterways by Alan Firth — Opened in 1794 with the completion of the Warwick + B'ham Canal, Shrewley Tunnel on the Grand Union Canal is remarkable for the separate tunnel for the towpath [illegible] main tunn[el]

pages.text:
has rained heavily all morning. After lunch back at the campsite, we have come in for John's end of course concert.

[photo: three mannequin heads wearing elaborate bonnets]

Boat women's hats with elaborate frills & loops of curtain at the back.
